response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of broad engineering experience, genuine high-throughput exposure (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), and current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, which fits the AI-native baseline this role assumes. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from his skill set and work history (his systems work is in DLang, his product work in TypeScript/Node), his distributed-systems depth is a few years old, and there is little evidence of Staff-scope technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination, or formal operational/on-call ownership. Worth a conversation to probe transferability, but not a clean fit on paper.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code-generation work matches the AI-native engineering environment
Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong TypeScript across multiple product roles, plus broad polyglot breadth and a solid testing/craftsmanship culture
concerns: No Python experience listed, despite it being a must-have skill
Limited evidence of Staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder management across functions, or driving decisions at scale
No explicit agile/scrum, on-call, or incident-response experience; deepest systems work was in DLang and ended in 2020
